provenance.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation

summary: U-Net is an encoder-decoder CNN for biomedical image segmentation with a contracting path, a symmetric expanding path, and skip connections via cropped feature-map concatenation. Trained with a pixel-wise weighted cross-entropy loss and heavy elastic-deformation augmentation.

modules.name: ContractingPath
modules.description: Repeated 3x3 unpadded conv + ReLU, 2x2 max pool stride 2, doubling channels

modules.name: ExpansivePath
modules.description: 2x2 up-conv halving channels, concat with cropped encoder feature map, two 3x3 conv + ReLU

modules.name: OutputLayer
modules.description: 1x1 conv mapping 64-component features to class count

modules.name: WeightedCrossEntropyLoss
modules.description: Pixel-wise softmax + weighted cross entropy with border-separation weight map

equations.desc: He weight initialization, N = incoming nodes

training.augmentation: Random elastic deformations (3x3 grid, 10px std Gaussian displacements, bicubic interp), shift, rotation, gray-value variation, dropout at end of contracting path

assumptions: Unpadded (valid) convolutions throughout, causing output smaller than input
ambiguities: Exact learning rate not specified in paper
Number of training epochs not stated; only ~10h on Titan GPU reported
